title: Google Maps API key committed in plaintext in PUBLIC repo (4 files + git history)
description: A real Google Maps API key (AIzaSyA9wz…, redacted) is committed in plaintext in MycosoftLabs/website (visibility=PUBLIC) across GOOGLE_API_KEYS_STATUS.md, MANUAL_SECURITY_STEPS.md, SECURITY_AUDIT_2026-03-11.md, and docs/CHANGELOG_2026-01-17.md, and is present in git history (first seen commit 753a7bb2). It is a NEXT_PUBLIC (client-exposed) Maps key, so browser exposure is by design, but a key committed to a public repo must be rotated and locked to HTTP referrers. GitHub push-protection/secret-scanning are enabled but did not flag it (Maps keys are outside GitHub's default secret patterns).
recommended_remediation: Rotate the Google Maps key in Google Cloud Console; apply HTTP-referrer + API restrictions to the new key; remove the value from all 4 tracked files; purge from git history (git filter-repo/BFG) since the repo is public. Replace with a build-time env var only.
poam_eligibility_basis: secret hygiene, not a 110-control weighted item

title: SECURITY DEFINER functions callable by authenticated role via REST (privilege-escalation surface)
description: On the active Supabase prod project (hnevnsxnhfibhbsipqvz), five SECURITY DEFINER functions are executable by the `authenticated` role via /rest/v1/rpc: handle_super_admin_role(), is_staff(), handle_new_user(), get_user_monthly_usage(), create_chain_entry(). handle_super_admin_role() and is_staff() are role/authorization functions that should not be directly invocable by end users — this is a potential privilege-escalation vector.
recommended_remediation: For each function, REVOKE EXECUTE from authenticated (and anon), or switch to SECURITY INVOKER, or move out of the exposed public schema. Trigger helpers (handle_new_user, handle_super_admin_role) should not be directly callable.

title: Overly-permissive RLS (WITH CHECK true) on audit_logs / security_events / incidents — audit integrity
description: Supabase prod RLS policies allow any `authenticated` user unrestricted INSERT into audit_logs, security_events, incidents, defense_briefing_requests, and unrestricted UPDATE of incidents (USING true AND WITH CHECK true). Any signed-in user can forge or alter audit-log, security-event, and incident records, undermining audit-record integrity and incident data integrity.

affected_asset.identifier: Supabase project hnevnsxnhfibhbsipqvz — public.audit_logs, security_events, incidents, defense_briefing_requests

recommended_remediation: Replace WITH CHECK (true) with tenant/owner-scoped predicates (e.g. auth.uid() = user_id). audit_logs and security_events should be INSERT-only via service_role or a SECURITY DEFINER writer, never directly writable by authenticated; incidents UPDATE must be constrained to owner/role. Prevents audit forgery.

title: No branch protection on production public repo main branch
description: MycosoftLabs/website (PUBLIC) main branch returns 'Branch not protected': no required pull-request reviews, no required status checks, force-push permitted, no signed-commit requirement. For a defense-contractor production site repo, this means changes can be pushed directly to main with no review or CI gate.
recommended_remediation: Enable branch protection on main: require >=1 PR review, required status checks, block force-push and deletion, enforce for admins, require signed commits.

title: Postgres functions with mutable search_path
description: Two Supabase prod functions (worldview_rate_weight_last_minute, worldview_meter_and_limit) have a role-mutable search_path, a known injection/privilege-escalation hardening gap for SECURITY DEFINER or trigger functions.
recommended_remediation: ALTER FUNCTION ... SET search_path = ''; (or a pinned schema list) on both functions.

title: Public Supabase storage buckets allow file listing
description: Public buckets `avatars` and `species-images` have a broad SELECT policy on storage.objects allowing clients to enumerate all files. Non-CUI content, but unnecessary information disclosure.
recommended_remediation: Scope the SELECT policy to object-URL access only (drop list capability) or make the bucket private with signed URLs.

title: Eight tables have RLS enabled but no policy (access model undefined)
description: tissue_access_grants, tissue_contaminations, tissue_events, tissue_experiment_accessions, tissue_interactions, tissue_locations, tissue_scientists, tissue_transfers have RLS enabled with zero policies. Effect is default-deny for anon/authenticated (safe), but indicates an incomplete access model — either the feature is unreachable except via service_role, or policies were never written.
recommended_remediation: Define explicit RLS policies matching the intended access model, or document that these tables are service_role-only by design.

title: Dependency vulnerabilities: 1 high, 10 moderate, 5 low (npm audit)
description: npm audit on website reports 16 vulnerabilities (1 high, 10 moderate, 5 low, 0 critical). The high is undici (TLS certificate validation bypass via dropped requestTls in SOCKS5 ProxyAgent). Real exploitability is low — the app does not use undici with a SOCKS5 ProxyAgent — but it is a transitive dep that should be updated.
recommended_remediation: Run npm audit fix; bump undici (likely transitive via Next.js) to a patched version; re-audit. Add npm audit / Dependabot to CI.

title: Two orphaned/paused Supabase projects alongside production
description: Org wxxqzjxruxkfwaoubtfl holds three projects: 'Mycosoft.com Production' (ACTIVE, pg17) plus 'mycosoft.com' and 'mycosoftmas' (both INACTIVE/paused, pg15, created 2025-04-15). Paused projects may still retain data-at-rest and represent orphaned attack surface / governance ambiguity.
recommended_remediation: Confirm no CUI/PII resides in the paused projects; export any needed data; delete the orphaned projects or document them in the system inventory (SSP Attachment A).

title: Build/temp artifacts committed to the public repo
description: Tracked files temp_original_crep.txt and tsc_output_utf8.txt are build/temp outputs committed to the public repo. They may leak internal file paths/structure and are repo hygiene debt.
recommended_remediation: git rm the temp/build artifacts and add matching .gitignore entries.

title: GitHub org 2FA enforcement — NOT VERIFIABLE with available access (requires operator)
description: Could not confirm org-wide 2FA enforcement, member list, OAuth-app grants, or PAT inventory for the MycosoftLabs org: the available gh token (account nodefather) lacks org-owner scope, so /orgs/MycosoftLabs admin fields returned empty. This is flagged Not Verified per the honesty rule, not assumed compliant.

evidence.path: gh api /orgs/MycosoftLabs -> admin fields empty (insufficient scope)

recommended_remediation: Operator with org-owner access: verify Settings > Authentication security > Require two-factor authentication = ON; export member + outside-collaborator + OAuth-app + PAT inventory; revoke classic PATs.
